plugin.name: Service Hooks
plugin.description: Plans deterministic service-hook rollouts and webhook delivery previews.

capabilities.description: Builds a predictable service-hook configuration plan from event inputs.
Renders a safe webhook delivery preview without making any network calls.
Publishes lifecycle and host-capability expectations for rollout tooling.

actions.label: Plan hook set
actions.description: Generate deterministic hook endpoints, retries, and ownership hints.

required.detail: Hosts must surface the generated rollout plan.

optional.detail: Rollout checklists render best as markdown.

actions.label: Preview delivery
actions.description: Show a webhook request preview with headers, payload, and retry timing.

required.detail: Hosts must surface the delivery envelope JSON.

optional.detail: HTTP request previews render best as verbatim code blocks.
optional.fallback: Hosts can fall back to JSON-only previews when code blocks are unavailable.

optional.detail: Async-capable hosts can treat preview generation as a background job.
optional.fallback: Foreground execution is still valid because the preview is deterministic.

notes: Useful for CLI demos, service orchestration smoke tests, and web panels that need structured hook metadata.
Every action is read-only and emits preview data only.
Phase 4 uses this plugin to demonstrate lifecycle, async metadata, trust signaling, and capability degradation in one runnable flow.

tested_hosts.notes: Foreground rollout planning is the default operator workflow.
Browser hosts can render preview cards with async orchestration.
Service hosts can reuse the JSON envelopes for health and rollout APIs.

notes: The payload and output shapes are additive so rollout tooling can tolerate new metadata fields.
Retry-preview semantics stay stable across host releases within the declared window.
The plugin does not contact live webhook endpoints.
Signature and retry artifacts are synthetic so hosts can replay them safely.

lifecycle.health_probe: Run preview-delivery with a canned deploy.succeeded payload and verify the generated retry envelope.
notes: The demo remains stateless, but hosts can still wire lifecycle hooks into rollout supervisors.
Shutdown hooks are metadata-only in this sample and exist to show production cleanup affordances.
Hosts may degrade async preview generation to foreground execution without changing output semantics.
Progress updates are optional metadata for hosts that visualize rollout preparation stages.

required.detail: Hosts must render or forward the structured rollout and delivery metadata.

optional.detail: Rollout checklists read best as markdown summaries.
optional.fallback: The JSON plan remains authoritative when markdown rendering is unavailable.

optional.detail: HTTP previews read best in code blocks.
optional.fallback: Hosts can fall back to JSON-only request envelopes.

optional.detail: Async-capable hosts can queue delivery previews as background work.
optional.fallback: Foreground execution stays safe because previews are deterministic and side-effect free.

optional.detail: Service-style hosts can wire the health probe into operational checks.
optional.fallback: Other hosts can document the health probe without executing it automatically.

degradation.behavior: If markdown-output is unavailable the plugin returns JSON only and omits checklist formatting.
If code-output is unavailable the host should surface the JSON delivery envelope instead of verbatim HTTP previews.
If async-jobs is unavailable the host should run preview-delivery in the foreground.

notes: Capability negotiation is intentionally declarative so host-cli inspect output stays readable.

release.installer_hint: Copy the dylib next to the manifest snapshot when bundling rollout previews for host-cli or service hosts.
